software_item.name: object-storage
software_item.description: File storage service for arbitrary objects (images, documents, text files) with AES-256 encryption at rest, organized into buckets with label-based metadata queries
software_item.safety_class_rationale: Supporting infrastructure component that stores configuration and non-clinical files. Does not directly process clinical data or make clinical decisions. Failure affects system availability but not immediate patient safety. Classification may change if clinical data is stored.

hazard_contributions.description: Encrypted object stored with corrupted or incorrect data encryption key
hazard_contributions.cause: Database transaction succeeds for metadata but DEK is not properly linked to ciphertext, or encryption key generation fails silently
hazard_contributions.potential_harm: Permanent data loss - object becomes unrecoverable as correct decryption key is unavailable

hazard_contributions.description: Key encryption key (KEK) exposed or leaked through logs or error messages
hazard_contributions.cause: KEK stored in memory could be exposed through error messages, logging, or memory dumps
hazard_contributions.potential_harm: All stored objects compromised - attacker with KEK can decrypt all data encryption keys and thus all objects

hazard_contributions.description: Object metadata saved but object file write fails
hazard_contributions.cause: Database transaction commits but filesystem write fails due to disk full, permission error, or timeout
hazard_contributions.potential_harm: Data inconsistency - metadata exists but object is missing, leading to 404 errors when object is retrieved

hazard_contributions.description: Unauthorized access to objects due to authentication bypass
hazard_contributions.cause: Basic auth credentials not validated, access control middleware misconfigured, or permission checks bypassed
hazard_contributions.potential_harm: Confidential configuration or clinical data exposed to unauthorized users

hazard_contributions.description: Filesystem timeout causes incomplete write or delete operations
hazard_contributions.cause: Filesystem operation exceeds configured timeout (default 5s), context cancelled mid-operation
hazard_contributions.potential_harm: Partial file writes or orphaned files - data corruption or resource exhaustion over time

hazard_contributions.description: SQL injection through label parameters in metadata queries
hazard_contributions.cause: Label values not properly escaped in dynamically constructed SQL queries (LIKE clauses with user input)
hazard_contributions.potential_harm: Database compromise - attacker could read/modify arbitrary data, extract encryption keys from database

hazard_contributions.description: Incorrect decryption key used for object retrieval
hazard_contributions.cause: Database query returns wrong metadata record, or KEK ID mismatch not caught
hazard_contributions.potential_harm: Decryption failure returns corrupted data to caller - could cause downstream processing errors

hazard_contributions.description: Database connection failure during critical operation
hazard_contributions.cause: MariaDB unavailable, network partition, connection pool exhausted
hazard_contributions.potential_harm: Service unavailable - dependent services cannot store/retrieve configuration, impacting overall system function

hazard_contributions.description: Cache returns stale or corrupted object data
hazard_contributions.cause: Cache eviction race condition, corruption in BigCache, or cache not invalidated on update
hazard_contributions.potential_harm: Stale configuration delivered to services - could cause incorrect system behavior

hazard_contributions.description: Insufficient error handling exposes internal system details
hazard_contributions.cause: Error messages include file paths, SQL queries, or internal state information
hazard_contributions.potential_harm: Information disclosure assists attackers in system reconnaissance

hazard_contributions.description: Notification service failure prevents audit trail
hazard_contributions.cause: ActiveMQ/ZeroMQ connection fails, message serialization error
hazard_contributions.potential_harm: Object creation events not logged - loss of audit trail for compliance and security monitoring

hazard_contributions.description: Rollback failure leaves orphaned object file after metadata error
hazard_contributions.cause: DeleteObject fails during rollback in saveObject error path
hazard_contributions.potential_harm: Disk space exhaustion from orphaned encrypted files over time

risk_controls_implemented.description: AES-256-GCM encryption for all objects at rest with per-object unique DEKs
risk_controls_implemented.implementation: Each object encrypted with unique 256-bit AES key using GCM mode. DEK never reused. KEK stored separately from database.

risk_controls_implemented.description: KEK stored only in memory, never persisted to database
risk_controls_implemented.implementation: KEK loaded from Kubernetes secret at startup, stored in unexported field to prevent accidental logging

risk_controls_implemented.description: KEK ID mismatch detection prevents wrong key usage
risk_controls_implemented.implementation: Decrypt function validates KEK ID matches DEK before attempting decryption

risk_controls_implemented.description: Cryptographically secure random number generator for DEK generation
risk_controls_implemented.implementation: Uses crypto/rand.Read for DEK generation, ensuring no key reuse or predictability

risk_controls_implemented.description: Rollback mechanism deletes orphaned object file if metadata save fails
risk_controls_implemented.implementation: saveObject attempts DeleteObject if ObjectMetadataRepo.Save fails, logs error if rollback fails

risk_controls_implemented.description: Basic authentication required for all API endpoints
risk_controls_implemented.implementation: AccessController middleware checks BasicAuth credentials before allowing resource access

risk_controls_implemented.description: Resource-based permission checks using Allower interface
risk_controls_implemented.implementation: Each endpoint specifies required permission (Read/Create/Delete) and resources

risk_controls_implemented.description: Filesystem operation timeout prevents indefinite blocking
risk_controls_implemented.implementation: All filesystem operations wrapped in context.WithTimeout (default 5s configurable)

risk_controls_implemented.description: Parameterized SQL queries for metadata operations
risk_controls_implemented.implementation: Direct queries use ? placeholders and db.QueryContext/ExecContext with args

risk_controls_implemented.description: Label value escaping for LIKE clauses in dynamic queries
risk_controls_implemented.implementation: escapeString function replaces SQL metacharacters before query construction

risk_controls_implemented.description: Caching with TTL to reduce filesystem load and improve performance
risk_controls_implemented.implementation: BigCache with configurable TTL (default 60min) and size limit (default 200MB), graceful degradation on cache failure

risk_controls_implemented.description: Structured error handling with error ID and status codes
risk_controls_implemented.implementation: httputil.Error wraps errors with unique ID, HTTP status, generic message. Internal error details not exposed to client.

risk_controls_implemented.description: Error logging for notification failures does not block object creation
risk_controls_implemented.implementation: CreateObject returns error if Notify fails, ensuring client is aware of audit trail gap

risk_controls_implemented.description: Bucket configuration validates required labels on object creation
risk_controls_implemented.implementation: Bucket.AssertLabels validates that all required labels are present before object is stored

risk_controls_implemented.description: Cache invalidation on delete prevents serving deleted objects
risk_controls_implemented.implementation: Delete operation removes entry from cache, logs warning on failure

risk_controls_implemented.description: Prometheus metrics for filesystem and database operations
risk_controls_implemented.implementation: Latency histograms and success/error counters for all repository operations

risk_controls_required_upstream.description: Kubernetes secret rotation policy for KEKs
risk_controls_required_upstream.rationale: Object-storage service cannot rotate its own KEK - requires orchestration-level coordination

risk_controls_required_upstream.description: User permission management and credential lifecycle
risk_controls_required_upstream.rationale: Service validates credentials but does not manage user creation, roles, or revocation
Service cannot implement its own database backup strategy - requires system-level DBA procedures

risk_controls_required_upstream.description: Monitoring and alerting on filesystem capacity
risk_controls_required_upstream.rationale: Service logs metrics but cannot implement alerting or capacity planning - requires system monitoring

risk_controls_required_upstream.description: Network security controls and TLS enforcement
risk_controls_required_upstream.rationale: Service implements application-level auth but cannot enforce network-level security policies

risk_controls_required_upstream.description: Audit log aggregation and retention policy
risk_controls_required_upstream.rationale: Service emits notification events but cannot guarantee delivery or retention - requires system-level log management

failure_modes.name: Encryption key generation fails
failure_modes.effect_on_software_item: CreateObject returns error, object not stored
failure_modes.effect_on_system: Caller receives 500 error, must retry or handle failure. Configuration upload fails.
failure_modes.detection_mechanism: Error returned to client, logged with zap logger, Prometheus metrics increment error counter
failure_modes.recovery_mechanism: Client retry, no persistent state change

failure_modes.name: Database connection lost during metadata save
failure_modes.effect_on_software_item: Save fails with database error, rollback triggered to delete object file
failure_modes.effect_on_system: Object creation fails, client receives error. Dependent services cannot store configuration.
failure_modes.detection_mechanism: Database driver error, logged, Prometheus query error metric incremented
failure_modes.recovery_mechanism: Client retry after database recovery, database reconnection pool handles transient failures

failure_modes.name: Filesystem write timeout exceeded
failure_modes.effect_on_software_item: Context cancelled, file write incomplete, error returned
failure_modes.effect_on_system: Object creation fails, possible orphaned partial file on disk
failure_modes.detection_mechanism: Context deadline exceeded error, logged, filesystem latency metric shows timeout
failure_modes.recovery_mechanism: Client retry, manual cleanup of orphaned files, increase FILESYSTEM_TIMEOUT_SECONDS if persistent

failure_modes.name: SQL injection via label parameter
failure_modes.effect_on_software_item: Attacker-controlled SQL executed, potential data exfiltration or modification
failure_modes.effect_on_system: Database compromised, all stored objects potentially readable by attacker
failure_modes.detection_mechanism: Database error logs, anomalous query patterns in metrics
failure_modes.recovery_mechanism: Manual incident response, key rotation, database audit, patch deployment

failure_modes.name: Authentication credentials missing or invalid
failure_modes.effect_on_system: Client cannot access resources, legitimate operations blocked if credentials misconfigured
failure_modes.recovery_mechanism: Client provides correct credentials, admin fixes user configuration

failure_modes.name: Decryption fails due to corrupted ciphertext
failure_modes.effect_on_software_item: GCM authentication tag mismatch, decryption fails
failure_modes.effect_on_system: GetObject returns error, caller cannot retrieve object, data effectively lost
failure_modes.detection_mechanism: Decryption error logged, 500 response to client
failure_modes.recovery_mechanism: Restore from backup if available, otherwise data lost. Investigate filesystem corruption.

failure_modes.name: Cache returns stale object after update
failure_modes.effect_on_software_item: Old version of object returned from cache instead of updated version from disk
failure_modes.effect_on_system: Caller receives outdated configuration, potential incorrect behavior
failure_modes.detection_mechanism: Checksum mismatch if caller validates, otherwise silent failure
failure_modes.recovery_mechanism: Cache TTL expires and fresh data loaded, or manual cache flush

failure_modes.name: Notification service unavailable during object creation
failure_modes.effect_on_software_item: Notify call fails, error returned from CreateObject
failure_modes.effect_on_system: Object creation fails, audit event not recorded, must retry entire operation
failure_modes.recovery_mechanism: Client retry after notification service recovers

failure_modes.name: Rollback delete fails after metadata save error
failure_modes.effect_on_software_item: Orphaned encrypted file left on disk, error logged but not returned
failure_modes.effect_on_system: Disk space gradually consumed by orphaned files
failure_modes.detection_mechanism: Error log entry, filesystem metrics show disk usage growth
failure_modes.recovery_mechanism: Manual cleanup job to identify and remove orphaned files, investigate root cause of delete failure

failure_modes.name: KEK ID mismatch between DEK and available KEKs
failure_modes.effect_on_software_item: Decrypt fails, KEK not found error
failure_modes.effect_on_system: GetObject fails, object unrecoverable if KEK permanently lost
failure_modes.recovery_mechanism: Restore missing KEK from backup, update KEK configuration

failure_modes.name: Bucket configuration missing or invalid
failure_modes.effect_on_software_item: All operations on bucket fail with BadRequest
failure_modes.recovery_mechanism: Fix buckets.yaml configuration and restart service

failure_modes.name: HTTP request body too large
failure_modes.effect_on_system: Service becomes unresponsive, impacts other requests
failure_modes.recovery_mechanism: Service restart, implement request size limit middleware

interfaces_with_safety_impact.safety_impact: Complete service failure if database unavailable. Metadata loss if database corrupted. All objects become unrecoverable without metadata.
interfaces_with_safety_impact.failure_handling: Database driver returns error, propagated to client as 500. Connection pool retries transient failures. No circuit breaker implemented.
interfaces_with_safety_impact.timeout: Not explicitly configured, uses driver defaults
interfaces_with_safety_impact.retry_policy: Connection pool automatic retry, no application-level retry

interfaces_with_safety_impact.safety_impact: Object data loss if filesystem fails or corrupted. Service cannot function without write access. Disk full prevents new object storage.
interfaces_with_safety_impact.failure_handling: Filesystem errors logged and returned as 500 errors. Context timeout (5s default) prevents indefinite blocking.
interfaces_with_safety_impact.retry_policy: No automatic retry, client must retry

interfaces_with_safety_impact.safety_impact: Audit trail gap if notification fails. Object creation fails entirely if notification cannot be sent.
interfaces_with_safety_impact.failure_handling: Notification failure causes CreateObject to fail and return error. No retry mechanism.
interfaces_with_safety_impact.timeout: Not visible in object-storage code, delegated to notification client

interfaces_with_safety_impact.safety_impact: Same as ITEM-OS-IF-003, alternative message queue implementation
If authentication service unavailable or returns incorrect results, unauthorized access possible or legitimate access blocked.
interfaces_with_safety_impact.failure_handling: Authentication failure returns 401/403 to client, operation blocked

interfaces_with_safety_impact.safety_impact: Primary interface for all operations. Malformed requests, missing auth, or protocol errors could cause service errors.
interfaces_with_safety_impact.failure_handling: Echo middleware handles request parsing errors, auth errors, and application errors with appropriate HTTP status codes
interfaces_with_safety_impact.timeout: HTTP server timeout (not explicitly configured, uses Echo defaults)
interfaces_with_safety_impact.retry_policy: Client-side retry, server does not retry

verification_gaps.description: SQL injection protection via escapeString not verified with test cases
verification_gaps.recommendation: Add unit tests with malicious SQL payloads in label values to verify escaping works correctly

verification_gaps.description: Rollback mechanism (saveObject delete on metadata error) not tested
verification_gaps.recommendation: Add integration test that forces metadata save failure and verifies object file is deleted

verification_gaps.description: Permission-based access control (Allower interface) not tested
verification_gaps.recommendation: Add integration tests verifying different user roles can/cannot access different resources

verification_gaps.description: No test coverage for notification service failure scenarios
verification_gaps.recommendation: Add test that mocks notification service failure and verifies CreateObject returns error

verification_gaps.description: Cache invalidation on delete not verified
verification_gaps.recommendation: Add test that creates object, caches it, deletes it, and verifies cache entry removed

verification_gaps.description: Database connection failure handling not tested
verification_gaps.recommendation: Add integration test using testcontainers to stop database and verify error handling

verification_gaps.description: No test for HTTP request body size limits
verification_gaps.recommendation: Add test with large request body to verify service behavior and implement body size limit

verification_gaps.description: Error message sanitization not verified to prevent information disclosure
verification_gaps.recommendation: Add tests that trigger various errors and verify responses do not leak internal details

shared_hazards: Authentication bypass hazards shared with all services using Allower interface
Database failure hazards shared with all services using MariaDB
Notification failure hazards shared with all services emitting audit events
system_level_controls_needed: Centralized key management and rotation policy
Database backup and disaster recovery procedures
Centralized audit log aggregation and retention
Incident response procedures for security breaches
